name: Amaretto Cheesecake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 9-inch microwavable quiche dish, shallow round baking dish or deep pie plate, melt butter at high (100% power) for 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Stir in almonds and sugar until evenly coated with butter.
2. Press the mixture into the bottom and sides of the dish. Microwave at high for 2 to 3 minutes or until firm. If necessary, rotate the dish during cooking.
3. Beat cream cheese and sugar until light. Beat in eggs one at a time. Add sour cream, 2 tablespoons Amaretto (or almond liqueur), vanilla, and almond extract. Beat until smooth. Pour into baked crust.
4. Microwave uncovered at medium (50% power) for 14 to 18 minutes or until cheesecake is almost set in the center. Rotate the dish partway through cooking if necessary. Cool on a countertop to room temperature, then cover and refrigerate until serving time.
5. Reserve some whole raspberries for garnish. Puree the remaining raspberries. Push through a sieve to remove seeds. Stir 2 tablespoons Amaretto (or almond liqueur) into the puree. Add sugar to taste.
6. To serve, spoon some sauce onto dessert plates. Place a slice of cheesecake on the sauce. Garnish with reserved berries.
If you prefer, use 1 cup graham cracker crumbs with 1/2 cup finely chopped almonds for the crust. Use a food processor and pulse to chop nuts to an even consistency, rather than a fine powder. You could also substitute strawberries for the raspberries. To omit liqueur, increase almond extract in the filling to 1 teaspoon and use just a drop in the raspberry puree.
